idea-plugin.name: TrAcerola
idea-plugin.description:
Breakpoint-driven test case generator for IntelliJ IDEA.

When paused at a breakpoint inside a Spring service or repository method, TrAcerola analyses the method body, discovers all injected dependencies (@Autowired / @Inject / @Resource), traces every external call made on those dependencies, and generates a JUnit 5 + Mockito test skeleton — complete with @Mock fields, when/thenReturn setup, method invocation, and assertion placeholders — ready for immediate review and editing.

How to use:
  1. Start a debug session and pause at a breakpoint inside any method.
  2. Right-click and choose Trace this Method, or use the debugger toolbar, Run menu, or press Ctrl+Alt+Shift+T.
  3. TrAcerola reads the actual runtime parameter values from the debugger, traces external dependency calls, and generates a test skeleton with real values.
  4. Click Insert into Test File to append the method to the existing test class (or create a new one), then run and refine.

action.description: Trace the paused method and generate a JUnit 5 + Mockito test with captured runtime values
